pattern: Telling a subagent to 'WAIT for SendMessage from sibling X'
why-bad: Subagents spawned via Agent tool are stateless one-shot workers with no inbox; they cannot wait; the workstream silently aborts
instead: Use memory-as-bus: Lead reads sibling output from memory, then spawns the next agent with explicit memory keys as inputs

pattern: Spawning N dependent agents in one batch expecting them to chain via peer messages
why-bad: No peer messaging mechanism exists; agents run open-loop on stale assumptions
instead: Spawn sequentially with Lead verifying upstream outputs in memory before each downstream spawn

pattern: Holding std::sync::Mutex across .await
why-bad: Blocks the async reactor; can deadlock the entire runtime under load
instead: Use tokio::sync::Mutex or refactor to release the lock before the await point

pattern: Holding tree-sitter WASM heap across many files without recycling worker thread
why-bad: WASM linear memory only grows, never shrinks; long-running indexes OOM
instead: Recycle the parse worker thread every N=250 files (see codegraph prior art) and impose a per-file parse timeout

pattern: Unbounded HashMap caches in cross-file reference resolution
why-bad: On 20k+ file repositories the caches consume gigabytes and OOM
instead: All resolution caches must be LRU-bounded; content cache gets 1/5 the budget of metadata caches

pattern: Adding single-column indexes when a composite index already covers the prefix
why-bad: Single-column indexes become dead weight on writes and provide no read advantage when the composite (col, kind) is already present
instead: Rely on left-prefix scan of the composite; drop redundant single-column indexes (see codegraph migration v4 prior art)

pattern: Letting any actor (human or AI) push directly to main or develop
why-bad: Breaks branch protection, audit trail, and the registry-first release contract
instead: All changes go through PR to develop; release/* and hotfix/* are the only paths to main

pattern: Adding language support by modifying core engine code
why-bad: Violates Charter §4; each ad-hoc core change makes adding the next language harder
instead: Express the new language entirely as files under packs/<lang>/ (pack.toml, queries.scm, optional hooks.wasm); if impossible, open an RFC to extend the pack interface itself

pattern: 同一个 commit 同时写实现和测试，而不是先写 RED 测试
why-bad: 违反 Charter §5.1；没有失败的测试就无法证明实现是有效的；自动循环在 RFC-0082~0088 图算法系列中全部犯了这个错误
instead: 严格顺序：(1) 写失败测试 → cargo test 确认 RED，(2) 写最小实现 → cargo test 确认 GREEN，(3) 重构，(4) 提交。实现代码和测试代码必须在同一 PR 但测试必须先于实现存在

pattern: 实现完成后不回填 RFC 验收标准复选框
why-bad: RFC 变成死文件；没有人知道哪些功能真正满足了验收标准；90 个 RFC 中 0 个验收标准被打勾
instead: 每个 PR 合并前必须在对应 RFC 中把已实现的验收标准 [ ] 改为 [x]；若所有标准都打勾，在 RFC header 中把 Status 改为 Implemented

pattern: 做了架构决策但没有写 ADR
why-bad: Charter §3 技术栈锁定了 15+ 个决策，但只有 3 个 ADR；Patricia Trie、MessagePack 格式、同步 reindex 等重要决策没有留存理由，后续 agent 无法理解为什么这么做
instead: 每次做影响公共 API、数据格式、性能策略、依赖选择的决策时，必须先写 ADR（docs/adr/NNNN-name.md），然后才提交代码

pattern: 自动循环无限往一个方向做，而不是做最高优先级的事
why-bad: 用户让循环全自动开发，结果循环做了 10 个图算法 RFC，完全忽略了最高优先级的 Hyphae DSL、Salsa 集成和基础架构工作
instead: 每次循环迭代开始前，必须读 PM 的最新 standup 或 decisions.jsonl 的最新条目，确认当前最高优先级，而不是默认继续做手边的事

pattern: Bellman-Ford最长路径在循环图上不加终止条件
why-bad: a→b→c→a的cycle导致queue永远增长，test永久hang；nextest超时10s发现
instead: 在每个节点的relaxation次数上加cap：if relax_count[node] >= n_symbols { continue }，保证O(V²)终止

pattern: cargo llvm-cov --workspace ... --fail-under-lines 90 --fail-under-branches 80
why-bad: cargo-llvm-cov 没有 --fail-under-branches 这个 flag；只有 --fail-under-lines。CI 整轮立刻失败，看上去像 coverage 真的没过。Branch coverage 要靠 Codecov 单独 gate。
instead: 只写 --fail-under-lines；branch 覆盖率上传到 Codecov。在加任何 fail-under-* flag 前，先在本地 cargo llvm-cov --help | grep fail-under 确认 flag 存在。

pattern: 在 .cargo/config.toml 加 [target.x86_64-pc-windows-msvc] rustflags = ["-C", "link-arg=/STACK:8388608"] 想修 Windows stack overflow
why-bad: GH Actions workflow 在 env block 设 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings' — 这会 FULLY 覆盖 .cargo/config.toml 的 rustflags（cargo 只在两边都来自 config 文件时才 concat；env 永远赢）。所以那个 link-arg 在 CI 上 silently 不生效，Windows 继续 STATUS_STACK_OVERFLOW (-1073741571)。
instead: 用 crate-level build.rs 输出 cargo:rustc-link-arg-bin=<binname>=/STACK:8388608。build.rs 的 link-arg 不受 RUSTFLAGS env override 影响，而且作用域限定到一个 binary。

pattern: DCO 用 git rev-list base..head 不加 --no-merges
why-bad: back-merge PRs（release/* → develop / main）的 rev-list 包含历史上的 PR-merge commits（如 'Merge pull request #164'），那些 merge commits 从来没带过 Signed-off-by trailer（且早于 DCO 强制要求），所以整轮 DCO 失败，看上去像有未签字的提交。
instead: 加 --no-merges 跳过 merge commits。DCO 只针对 author commits 强制，merge commits 是 git 自动产物。

pattern: 在 security job 里同时跑 cargo-deny check + cargo-audit
why-bad: cargo-deny 的 [advisories] section 已经覆盖了 RustSec advisory check（和 cargo-audit 重复）。但 cargo-audit 每次都重新 clone advisory-db (github.com/RustSec/advisory-db)，GH runner 出站网络经常 failed to prepare clone — 持续 RED；3x retry 也无效。
instead: 留 cargo-deny，删 cargo-audit。advisory 覆盖不变；CI 稳定。cargo-deny 用自己的 cached fetch path 比 cargo-audit 健壮。

pattern: release.yml 写 'uses: ./.github/workflows/ci.yml' 但 ci.yml 的 on: block 没有 workflow_call:
why-bad: workflow 直接 fail 显示 'This run likely failed because of a workflow file issue.' 而不告诉你具体原因。release 流程整个跑不起来。
instead: 被复用的 workflow 必须在 on: 加 workflow_call:（可以同时有 pull_request、push、workflow_dispatch、workflow_call —— 互不冲突）。

pattern: 在 GH Actions step 用 grep '^version =' Cargo.toml 提取 workspace 版本
why-bad: 我们的 Cargo.toml 用对齐空白 'version       = "0.1.4"'，单个空格的 grep 不匹配，validate 立刻失败。
instead: 用 POSIX 字符类 grep -E '^version[[:space:]]*='，sed 同步用 '[[:space:]]*'。BSD sed / GNU sed 都认。

pattern: test 用 Path::new(env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR")).ancestors().find(|p| p.join("Cargo.lock").exists()) 找 workspace root
why-bad: 在 cargo package / vendor layout 下，单 crate 自带 Cargo.lock，find 在 crates/<name>/ 就停了，walker 只看到那个 crate 的源（46 files）而不是全 workspace（145 files）。
instead: 用 structural path：crate 一定住在 <workspace>/crates/<name>/，所以 Path::new(env!(...)).parent().and_then(|p| p.parent()) 是 workspace root。

pattern: admin-merge PR 但 CI 还是 RED
why-bad: founder 立刻发现 ('CI 错误')，失去信任。--admin flag 绕过保护规则但不会让 CI 变绿；以后排查时 dev 不知道这个 commit 是不是经过 CI 验证的。
instead: CI 真绿才 admin-merge。Quality Gate FAILURE 时即使可以 --admin，也不要。诊断失败 → 修 → 等绿 → merge。

pattern: Creating a fix branch before reading decisions.jsonl to the end
why-bad: A previous agent may have already fixed the issue. Created duplicate branch fix/260-packs-divergence for issue #260 before discovering PRs #263/#264 already fixed it. Wasted ~8 minutes of the 25-minute window.
instead: Always tail -5 .hive/memory/decisions.jsonl (or read the last 10+ entries) before creating any fix branch. If an issue number appears in recent decisions as 'closed' or 'fixed', verify on GitHub before acting.

pattern: grep -q inside set -o pipefail pipeline with large upstream (git show on 11K-line file)
why-bad: grep -q exits early on first match; this sends SIGPIPE to upstream (git show). With pipefail, the upstream's SIGPIPE exit code (141) propagates and makes the if-condition FALSE even though grep found the match. The hook falsely reports TDD violation.
instead: Wrap in subshell with pipefail disabled: (set +o pipefail; git show ... | grep -q ...). Applied to scripts/install-hooks.sh line 89 and .git/hooks/pre-commit.

pattern: GitHub squash-merge of a PR drops the Signed-off-by (DCO) trailer from the resulting squash commit
why-bad: The squashed commit lands on develop without DCO; later when develop flows into a release/* branch, the release->main DCO gate (correctly, --no-merges) fails on that immutable commit, blocking the release. Happened with cecb11f (PR #331) blocking v0.1.14.
instead: Either (a) configure the squash-merge to use the PR commit message body which carries Signed-off-by, or (b) add a DCO-CI tolerance for commits whose body references a merged PR number, or (c) require DCO trailer in the PR title/squash template. Until fixed, release DCO failures from squash artifacts are a known founder-override case, not a real sign-off gap.

pattern: auto-commit-per-op on a multi-table KV store — each sub-operation (write_fwd, write_rev, trunk/kind/span) opens its own redb WriteTransaction and commits, with Results discarded via `let _ =`
why-bad: Breaks crash-atomicity: a logical op spanning N tables = N independent fsync'd commits; a crash/SIGKILL/disk-full between them leaves a torn state the in-memory oracle can never produce (one-directional edge: outgoing has it, incoming doesn't; or a ghost node with no edges). ALSO a perf cliff: 300K edges = ~1.2M fsyncs vs Charter §2 <30s. ALSO swallows transient IO errors silently in production, not just on crash.
instead: Wrap each LOGICAL operation in ONE redb WriteTransaction (upsert_edge = fwd+rev in one txn; remove_node = edges+trunk+kind+span in one txn). Extract private *_in(&WriteTransaction,...) helpers shared by auto-commit and a batched WriteBatch API so prod and test share ONE code path. Propagate commit() Results. Found by adversarial review of shipped RFC-0100 Phase 1 RedbBackend (wf_21a3635f-0e6, redb_backend.rs:446-480).

pattern: Treating a public GitHub tag or GitHub Release as proof that a release completed
why-bad: GitHub artifacts can be created before main/develop merges or registry publish actually succeed, leaving an orphan version that looks shipped but is unreachable from branches and unavailable to users on crates.io.
instead: Use the Charter §5.12 four-step ceremony as the only completion definition: main merge, tag, all five crates on crates.io, and develop back-merge. If any step is missing, stop release work, open/update a repair issue, and fix automation before retrying.

pattern: Using rg inside CI guardrail scripts without installing ripgrep
why-bad: The developer machine has rg, but GitHub's Ubuntu runner for lightweight governance jobs does not guarantee it. The new governance job failed before testing the release rule it was meant to protect.
instead: Use shell built-ins, grep, awk, sed, or install the dependency explicitly in the workflow. For tiny static checks, prefer grep so the guardrail has no tool bootstrap dependency.

pattern: Seeding a cached aggregate from the current post-mutation table state and then applying the same mutation delta again
why-bad: For legacy redb files opened through `open_existing()` without the new `meta.edge_count` key, the first write scans `synapse_fwd` after the edge has already been inserted or removed. Adding the delta on top double-counts inserts and under-counts removals.
instead: When the cached aggregate key is missing inside a write transaction, write the scan result as the current baseline and return without applying delta. Apply deltas only when the cache key already existed before the mutation.

pattern: treating an ownership index match as sufficient proof that underlying adjacency tables are already consistent
why-bad: Public storage operations can change adjacency without updating file ownership metadata, so a no-op shortcut can skip a repair that replace_file previously performed.
instead: Before returning early from a file-scoped redb replacement, verify the actual forward and reverse adjacency records for every owned edge in addition to file_index/trunk/metadata equality.

pattern: Test asserts platform-specific behavior unconditionally across all platforms
why-bad: measure_rss() documented returning None on Windows but test asserted is_some() unconditionally — caused Windows CI failure that blocked a release PR. Hard to detect locally (Linux/macOS devs never see it).
instead: Mirror the cfg gates from the implementation: use #[cfg(target_os = "macos")] / #[cfg(target_os = "linux")] on the positive-case test, and a complementary #[cfg(not(...))] test for the None case. The test set should cover 100% of cfg branches.

pattern: Inferring crates.io publish success from the presence of a develop back-merge commit
why-bad: The back-merge commit only proves the release branch was merged to develop; the crates.io publish step in release.yml can fail independently (and has failed on every release since v0.1.6 due to CRATES_IO_TOKEN/cargo-search issues). Declaring ceremony 'COMPLETE' from indirect evidence creates false confidence and leaves users unable to install the version via cargo.
instead: Check the release.yml workflow run status directly (GitHub Actions → Release workflow) for the specific commit. Only mark Step 3 complete when 'publish to crates.io' job shows SUCCESS and the version is visible at https://crates.io/crates/mycelium-rcig-core.
